title: Selinum tilingia
intro: Selinum tilingia, also known as Tiling's caraway, is a perennial plant native to the Himalayas and other parts of Asia, belonging to the carrot family (Apiaceae). The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antioxidant properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of digestive issues, fever, and respiratory problems. Bioactive constituents of Selinum tilingia include essential oils, flavonoids, and alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is commonly prepared as a decoction or infusion, and may also be used to make teas, tinctures, and essential oil extracts.
taxonomy: Selinum tilingia is classified in the family Apiaceae, commonly known as the carrot or parsley family. This family consists of a wide variety of plants, including carrots, parsley, and many others. The genus Selinum is a part of this family and is native to various parts of the world. Within the genus, Selinum tilingia is often placed in the tribe Apieae. The classification of Selinum tilingia can also be specified at the species level using binomial nomenclature, which includes both the genus and species names.
